questions.question: Find out the Normality of a solution, when 9.8 gms. of $\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4$ is dissolved in 500 ml solution.
questions.explanation: Normality is calculated using the formula $N = \frac{W \times 1000}{E \times V(ml)}$, where $E$ is the equivalent weight. For $\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4$, the equivalent weight is $\text{Molar Mass}/2 = 98/2 = 49$. Substituting the values, $N = \frac{9.8 \times 1000}{49 \times 500} = 0.4 \text{ N}$.

questions.question: How many grams of $\text{NaOH}$ will be required to prepare $500 \text{ gram}$ solution containing $10\% \text{ w/w NaOH}$ solution ?
questions.explanation: The percentage weight by weight ($\% \text{ w/w}$) is defined as the mass of solute in grams present in $100 \text{ g}$ of solution. For a $10\% \text{ w/w}$ solution, $100 \text{ g}$ of solution contains $10 \text{ g}$ of $\text{NaOH}$. Therefore, $500 \text{ g}$ of solution will contain $\frac{10 \times 500}{100} = 50 \text{ g}$ of $\text{NaOH}$.

questions.question: How many grams of Sulphuric acid is to be dissolved to prepare $200\text{ ml.}$ aqueous solution having concentration of $[\text{H}_3\text{O}^+]$ ions $1\text{ M}$ at $25^{\circ}\text{C}$ temperature. [H=1, O=16, S=32 gram . mole$^{-1}$ ]
questions.explanation: Since $\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4$ is a diprotic acid, it releases two $\text{H}_3\text{O}^+$ ions per molecule, meaning the molarity of the acid is half the concentration of $\text{H}_3\text{O}^+$ ions, which is $0.5 \text{ M}$. The mass required is calculated as $\text{Mass} = \text{Molarity} \times \text{Molar Mass} \times \text{Volume (L)}$. Thus, $\text{Mass} = 0.5 \times 98 \times 0.2 = 9.8 \text{ g}$.

questions.question: What is the normality of aqueous solution of $\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4$ having $\text{pH} = 1$.
questions.explanation: The $\text{pH}$ of a solution is given by $-\log[\text{H}_3\text{O}^+]$, so a $\text{pH}$ of $1$ corresponds to $[\text{H}_3\text{O}^+] = 10^{-1} = 0.1 \text{ M}$. In an aqueous solution of an acid, the normality is equal to the molar concentration of $\text{H}_3\text{O}^+$ ions. Therefore, the normality of the $\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4$ solution is $0.1 \text{ N}$.

questions.question: $1\text{ mole}$ of metal ‘M’ reacts completely with alcohol to give $1.5\text{ moles}$ of $\text{H}_2$. Then what will be the valency of metal ‘M’?
questions.explanation: The reaction of a metal $\text{M}$ with alcohol can be represented as $\text{M} + n\text{ROH} \rightarrow \text{M(OR)}_n + \frac{n}{2}\text{H}_2$, where $n$ is the valency of the metal. Given that $1 \text{ mole}$ of metal produces $1.5 \text{ moles}$ of $\text{H}_2$, we set $\frac{n}{2} = 1.5$. Solving for $n$ gives a valency of $3$.
